name: Arc Browser
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of The Browser Company's Arc brand language — the post-Chrome browser that treats the address bar as the enemy. The brand canvas is a soft warm off-white (#f5f3ed) with deep ink, or alternatively a watercolor-gradient hero band sweeping pastel coral, peach, and lavender across a hand-drawn marketing page. The voice is wide-eyed earnest tech-utopianism — sentence-case headlines, generous serif accents (Söhne or Inter for body, with proprietary curvilinear display nameplates), and animation that feels closer to children's illustration than browser-chrome. Arc is the brand where motion is the marketing.

design_language.motion_signature: Constant gentle motion is the brand. Hero gradients drift slowly with watercolor-bleed transitions (~12s loop). UI mockups float with subtle 4px vertical bobs over 3s ease-in-out. Hover states scale 1.02x with a 200ms cubic-bezier. Page transitions use slow 600ms fade-with-translate, never abrupt cuts.

trademark_signals: Watercolor-gradient hero — peach + coral + lavender pastels that bleed into each other like wet paint, used as full-bleed hero backgrounds beneath sentence-case display headlines
Sentence-case display headlines (never ALL CAPS, never Title Case) — 'Browse the web like a person' reads like a manifesto, not a marketing tagline
Curvilinear hand-drawn UI mockups — sidebar tabs render with rounded organic edges, not the chrome-rectangular grid every other browser uses
Color-personalization theme: every Arc screenshot shows a different user's chosen color palette — the chrome is meant to be expressive, not corporate
Long-scroll marketing pages narrated like a short film — each section reveals a new feature with a custom animated UI mockup, no static screenshots
ai_slop_avoided: Don't use Title Case or ALL CAPS headlines. Sentence-case is the brand voice — 'Browse the web like a person', not 'Browse The Web Like A Person'
Don't paint hero gradients with hard linear stops. The watercolor effect — soft bleed transitions, painterly edges — is the signature
Don't render browser UI mockups in flat rectangular Material/Fluent geometry. Arc UI mockups have organic curves, generous corners, and hand-drawn warmth
Don't show one corporate color theme across all screenshots. Every Arc screenshot should pick a different user-chosen palette — variety IS the personalization message
Don't strip the long-scroll narrative. Arc landing pages walk through features as a guided tour with custom animations; a single screenshot grid reads as off-brand
